notes.author: Buddy Williams
notes.body: Temporarily parked after automation claimed this implementation during the contract Goal review-to-revision interval. Do not resume until contract Goal 00000SZ2HZ9S31P6FH65XVA00C is approved and merged. The interrupted worktree is not authoritative and must be reconciled against the final contract before implementation.

notes.author: Buddy Williams
notes.body: Contract dependency satisfied: Goal 00000SZ2HZ9S31P6FH65XVA00C is done and merged at 6b9638c. Resumed as Round 2 so implementation starts from the merged contract rather than the interrupted pre-contract Round 1 branch.

rounds.prompt: Implement the shared workflow consistency and durable-evidence contract produced by the preceding ordered Goal. Reuse the existing Process, Workflow, operation registry, capacity, Goal/state, Git/worktree, and observability capabilities; do not add a second orchestration system or surface-specific control path. Establish one explicit cross-process coordination model that every authoritative workflow-state writer participates in, or equivalently version and revalidate all authoritative evidence so stale writers cannot resurrect or contradict terminal state. Preserve durable target-app ownership across app switches and detach, make operation, claim, process, capacity, Goal evidence, repository evidence, and logs converge under cancellation, recovery, restart, and late settlement, and define crash-safe persistence and failure recovery consistent with the contract. Include compatible migration for existing durable records. Exercise production-shaped multi-process paths with the real daemon/workflow runner and shared APIs, including concurrent mutation, cancellation, provider completion/failure, app switch, restart/crash, write/log failure, and capacity release; use the smallest focused evidence that proves the invariants. Keep UI, CLI, API, and MCP thin and semantically consistent. Treat the parked branches aafdc696 and c1e9a9a as prototypes and evidence only; do not silently merge their Supervisor- or Quality-specific behavior. After this foundation is reviewed and merged, those existing Goals will resume on top of it.

failed_actions.message: Authoritative workflow claims and decisions remain in port-scoped runtime_root/workflow-automation-state.json. Changing or discarding the runtime root forks or loses claim authority, mixing durable target-app workflow state with runtime artifacts. Legacy claims are also assigned to whichever target app is attached during migration, rather than preserving proven ownership.
failed_actions.rule: Keep authoritative target-app state local, durable, inspectable, and outside the primary source worktree; keep runtime artifacts separate and projections or caches rebuildable. State-layout changes require a compatible migration or explicit transition plan.

failed_actions.message: start_claim durably marks a claim Running before prepare_started_claim moves its Goal from todo. A crash between those steps leaves a Running claim attached to a todo Goal. Startup recovery ignores todo Goals, and scheduling launches only Claimed claims, so the orphaned claim is never recovered or executed.
failed_actions.rule: Keep the daemon and surfaces responsive by running agent, Git, quality, lifecycle, and other long-running work through the supervised process substrate with explicit ownership, status, logs, cancellation, failure feedback, and recovery.

failed_actions.message: Operation terminal settlement has contradictory authoritative paths: finish_with_result persists its completion log before state, while succeed_with_result_and_progress persists Succeeded without evidence and the Jira runner appends completion evidence afterward. A log failure therefore leaves a durable successful operation without its required completion evidence.
failed_actions.rule: Compose the existing Process, Agents, Workflow, Git, state, quality, governance, and evidence capabilities before creating new machinery. Each concern should have one authoritative implementation and source of truth unless the Goal explicitly proposes an architectural replacement.

failed_actions.message: The new concurrency, persistence, cancellation, and recovery behavior is covered only by in-process unit tests. The unchanged daemon-backed full-workflow test covers a happy path, and the multi-instance test only synchronizes sequential Goal creation; neither exercises concurrent mutation, cancellation versus provider settlement, crash recovery, app switching, or write/log failure through the real daemon and workflow runner.
failed_actions.rule: Require evidence proportional to actual architectural and operational risk. Verify the affected behavior with the smallest focused checks that provide confidence, including integration or failure-path evidence when a change alters concurrency, persistence, process, Git, release, recovery, or cross-surface semantics. Update only affected docs/intent pages when documented product intent or an architectural contract changes; do not require unrelated documentation or broad cleanup.

rounds.implementation_report:
Implemented and committed as `ba537ca` (`Implement shared workflow coordination`). The worktree is clean and reviewable.

Key changes:

- Added shared cross-process locking and crash-safe durable replacement.
- Added Goal/workflow revisions with legacy migration and stale-writer rejection.
- Persisted stable target-app, Goal round, claim, execution, and decision identities.
- Made cancellation authoritative over late provider settlement and retained capacity while processes drain.
- Required durable evidence before dependent Goal or operation state.
- Coordinated Git synchronization so unrelated records still converge during conflicts.
- Kept UI, CLI, API, and MCP paths unchanged as thin shared-capability adapters.

Final deterministic verification:

- `git diff --check && cargo fmt --all -- --check` — passed.
- `cargo check --all-targets` — passed.
- `cargo test --lib --no-fail-fast -- --test-threads=1` — 422 passed, 0 failed.
- `cargo run --manifest-path xtask/Cargo.toml -- test-full-workflow` — 1 passed, 0 failed.
- `cargo run --manifest-path xtask/Cargo.toml -- test-multi-instance-sync` — 1 passed, 0 failed.
- Final `git status --short` — clean.

Goal tracker completed in approximately 47 minutes.
rounds.prompt: The minimal shared workflow consistency contract from Goal 00000SZ2HZ9S31P6FH65XVA00C is now approved and merged on main at 6b9638c. Implement the original Round 1 objective fully against that contract and current main. Preserve its implementation scope and production-shaped concurrency, cancellation, provider settlement, target-app switch, restart/crash, persistence/log failure, capacity, and thin-surface evidence requirements, but choose the simplest coherent mechanism that satisfies the Intent invariants rather than recreating the discarded detailed documentation recipe. Reconcile or ignore the interrupted clean Round 1 worktree; do not treat it, aafdc696, or c1e9a9a as authoritative or merge them wholesale. Use existing Process, Workflow, operation, capacity, Goal/state, Git/worktree, and observability capabilities. Keep this implementation grounded in shared capability truth and leave Quality and Supervisor-specific behavior parked until this foundation is reviewed and merged.

Repair only the four post-implementation governance violations from Round 2 while preserving ba537ca as review evidence, not as merged authority. Start from current main and reconcile/cherry-pick the prior candidate as useful, then: (1) move authoritative workflow claims and decisions out of port-scoped runtime artifacts into target-app-local durable state outside the primary source worktree, with a compatible migration that never guesses legacy target-app ownership; keep runtime files rebuildable; (2) eliminate or recover the crash window where a claim is durably Running while its Goal remains todo, with deterministic restart evidence; (3) make operation terminal settlement use one authoritative evidence-before-state path, including Jira export, so log/write failure cannot publish durable success; and (4) extend production-shaped daemon/workflow verification to exercise concurrent mutation, cancellation versus late provider settlement, crash recovery, target-app switching, and write/log failure through real supervised paths. Keep surfaces thin, avoid a second scheduler/recovery engine, preserve confirmation and audit boundaries, and do not force merges or discard worktrees. Run focused tests plus the serial library gate and real workflow/multi-instance harnesses, then commit a clean scoped candidate.

failed_actions.message: The migration cannot import actual pre-change workflow records. Before this change, target_app_id was the target-root path; migrate_legacy_state only accepts records whose target_app_id equals the new app-<hash> identity. Existing claims and pause decisions are therefore silently left in runtime files while new durable target-app authority starts empty, with no explicit reconciliation plan.
failed_actions.rule: Keep authoritative target-app state local, durable, inspectable, and outside the primary source worktree; keep runtime artifacts separate and projections or caches rebuildable. State-layout changes require a compatible migration or explicit transition plan.

failed_actions.message: WorkflowEngine::retry acquires capacity and publishes the claim as Running with a new execution ID, but it launches no supervised process and execute_claimed_work only launches Claimed records. The retry API therefore reports a running execution that has no worker or process and retains its capacity lease indefinitely.
failed_actions.rule: Keep the daemon and surfaces responsive by running agent, Git, quality, lifecycle, and other long-running work through the supervised process substrate with explicit ownership, status, logs, cancellation, failure feedback, and recovery.

failed_actions.message: The added daemon-backed test does not run its cancellation race through the real workflow runner or provider process: it directly calls WorkflowEngine::claim/start_claim and constructs WorkflowContext in the test process. The full-workflow test remains a happy path, and no test exercises retry execution, allowing the durable Running-without-process defect to pass.
failed_actions.rule: Require evidence proportional to actual architectural and operational risk. Verify the affected behavior with the smallest focused checks that provide confidence, including integration or failure-path evidence when a change alters concurrency, persistence, process, Git, release, recovery, or cross-surface semantics. Update only affected docs/intent pages when documented product intent or an architectural contract changes; do not require unrelated documentation or broad cleanup.

rounds.implementation_report:
Completed and committed as `19461ba` (`Complete shared workflow consistency`) on top of the preserved Round 2 candidate.

What changed:

- Moved workflow authority into durable target-app Refine state; runtime files are projections only. Migration requires stable target identity and never guesses ownership.
- Added deterministic restart repair for Running claims whose Goals remain todo.
- Unified operation settlement so evidence is durable before terminal state, including Jira exports.
- Preserved Git conflicts across blind retries without advancing the failed baseline; unrelated authorities still converge, and supervisor state is explicitly non-authoritative.
- Expanded the two-daemon harness for concurrent mutation, repeated conflict, explicit revision resolution, cancellation/late settlement, runtime switching, restart recovery, and log-write failure ordering.

Verification:

- `git diff --check` — passed
- `cargo fmt --all -- --check` — passed
- `cargo check --all-targets` — passed
- `cargo test --lib --no-fail-fast -- --test-threads=1` — 427 passed, 0 failed
- `cargo run --manifest-path xtask/Cargo.toml -- test-full-workflow` — 1 passed, 0 failed
- `cargo run --manifest-path xtask/Cargo.toml -- test-multi-instance-sync` — 1 passed, 0 failed
- Final Git status — clean and reviewable
rounds.prompt:
Preserve and build on the verified Round 2 candidate ba537ca rather than replacing it. Correct only the remaining shared-consistency gaps.

1. Move authoritative workflow claims and decisions out of port-scoped runtime artifacts into durable target-app Refine state, with stable target-app ownership. Runtime files may remain only as rebuildable projections or process-local state. Provide a compatible migration that does not assign legacy claims to whichever app happens to be attached and does not silently fork or discard authority when runtime roots or ports change.
2. Make claiming and the Goal's todo-to-in-progress transition one recoverable commitment. A crash after marking a claim Running but before changing the Goal must not strand an unlaunchable claim; startup/reconciliation must deterministically repair every partial state.
3. Use one authoritative terminal-operation settlement path everywhere, including Jira export. Required completion/failure evidence must be durable before dependent terminal state; a log failure must not leave an operation durably successful.
4. Fix Git synchronization so a true same-authority conflict, especially two nodes changing the same Goal revision, remains a conflict on every blind/automatic retry until a new authoritative decision resolves it. A failed sync must not advance its baseline so unchanged stale local content becomes authoritative on the next pulse. Keep unrelated remote authorities converging despite a conflict, and treat explicitly derived non-authoritative projections such as supervisor-agent state as projections rather than workflow authority.
5. Add proportionate daemon-backed regression evidence for these operational risks: concurrent authoritative mutation; repeated same-Goal Git conflict without another local mutation; cancellation racing late provider settlement; crash/restart recovery of a partial claim; target-app or runtime-root switching; and write/log failure ordering. Keep the real full-workflow and two-daemon sync harnesses green.

Retain Round 2's stable target identity, optimistic revisions, durable replacement, capacity draining, cancellation guard, evidence ordering, and unrelated-state convergence. Do not expand into Quality behavior, Supervisor behavior, UI work, or unrelated cleanup.

failed_actions.message: WorkflowEngine::claim, promote, retry, and start_claim read Goal revision and eligibility before save_state, but save_state revalidates only workflow-state version. A concurrent Goal mutation can therefore commit first and still be followed by a stale Claimed or Running record instead of rejecting the stale workflow decision.
failed_actions.rule: Compose the existing Process, Agents, Workflow, Git, state, quality, governance, and evidence capabilities before creating new machinery. Each concern should have one authoritative implementation and source of truth unless the Goal explicitly proposes an architectural replacement.

failed_actions.message: On first observing a target, each workflow runner calls fail_interrupted_goals, which fails every active-stage Goal owned by its local Node without proving that the claim's supervised provider process is absent. Starting another daemon/runtime for the same Node therefore marks work still running under the first daemon Failed and interrupts its live claim.
failed_actions.rule: Keep the daemon and surfaces responsive by running agent, Git, quality, lifecycle, and other long-running work through the supervised process substrate with explicit ownership, status, logs, cancellation, failure feedback, and recovery.

failed_actions.message: state_conflicts suppresses a real multi-node conflict whenever the local workflow_revision is numerically greater than the remote revision. Revision magnitude does not establish that the local edit followed conflict review, so two offline local edits silently override one concurrent remote edit instead of preserving both decisions for resolution.
failed_actions.rule: Coordinate parallel or distributed work explicitly through Node ownership, workflow claims and capacity, dependency or Feature ordering, and isolated Git branches or worktrees so concurrent changes remain reviewable and recoverable.

failed_actions.message: The two-daemon test enables workflow automation on only one daemon and stops it before starting the recovery daemon; it never exercises simultaneous workflow runners or recovery while another runtime owns a live provider. Conflict tests cover equal revisions and an explicit post-conflict edit, but not unequal revisions produced concurrently, leaving both defects unverified.
failed_actions.rule: Require evidence proportional to actual architectural and operational risk. Verify the affected behavior with the smallest focused checks that provide confidence, including integration or failure-path evidence when a change alters concurrency, persistence, process, Git, release, recovery, or cross-surface semantics. Update only affected docs/intent pages when documented product intent or an architectural contract changes; do not require unrelated documentation or broad cleanup.

Update only affected docs/intent pages when documented product intent or an architectural contract changes; do not require unrelated documentation or broad cleanup.

rounds.governance_message: Found four concrete violations in coordination, recovery, distributed conflict handling, and verification evidence.

governance_rule_actions.message: WorkflowEngine::claim, promote, retry, and start_claim read Goal revision and eligibility before save_state, but save_state revalidates only workflow-state version. A concurrent Goal mutation can therefore commit first and still be followed by a stale Claimed or Running record instead of rejecting the stale workflow decision.
governance_rule_actions.rule: Compose the existing Process, Agents, Workflow, Git, state, quality, governance, and evidence capabilities before creating new machinery. Each concern should have one authoritative implementation and source of truth unless the Goal explicitly proposes an architectural replacement.

governance_rule_actions.message: On first observing a target, each workflow runner calls fail_interrupted_goals, which fails every active-stage Goal owned by its local Node without proving that the claim's supervised provider process is absent. Starting another daemon/runtime for the same Node therefore marks work still running under the first daemon Failed and interrupts its live claim.
governance_rule_actions.rule: Keep the daemon and surfaces responsive by running agent, Git, quality, lifecycle, and other long-running work through the supervised process substrate with explicit ownership, status, logs, cancellation, failure feedback, and recovery.

governance_rule_actions.message: state_conflicts suppresses a real multi-node conflict whenever the local workflow_revision is numerically greater than the remote revision. Revision magnitude does not establish that the local edit followed conflict review, so two offline local edits silently override one concurrent remote edit instead of preserving both decisions for resolution.
governance_rule_actions.rule: Coordinate parallel or distributed work explicitly through Node ownership, workflow claims and capacity, dependency or Feature ordering, and isolated Git branches or worktrees so concurrent changes remain reviewable and recoverable.

governance_rule_actions.message: The two-daemon test enables workflow automation on only one daemon and stops it before starting the recovery daemon; it never exercises simultaneous workflow runners or recovery while another runtime owns a live provider. Conflict tests cover equal revisions and an explicit post-conflict edit, but not unequal revisions produced concurrently, leaving both defects unverified.
governance_rule_actions.rule: Require evidence proportional to actual architectural and operational risk. Verify the affected behavior with the smallest focused checks that provide confidence, including integration or failure-path evidence when a change alters concurrency, persistence, process, Git, release, recovery, or cross-surface semantics. Update only affected docs/intent pages when documented product intent or an architectural contract changes; do not require unrelated documentation or broad cleanup.

rounds.implementation_report:
Completed and committed as `4da89bd` (`Complete supervised workflow coordination`). Worktree is clean and reviewable.

Changes:

- Migrates path-owned legacy workflow records to stable app IDs across runtime roots.
- Distinguishes owned, foreign, and ambiguous authority; ambiguous active claims or pause decisions block automation without creating competing state.
- Retry now creates a durable `Claimed` commitment, requires a managed workflow runner, preserves prior execution history, and acquires capacity only when scheduled.
- Explicit retry records evidence before rolling interrupted in-progress Goals back to `todo`.
- Daemon coverage now exercises cancellation versus late provider success and retry through the real workflow runner and managed provider process, including terminal settlement and capacity release.

Verification:

- `git diff --check` — passed.
- `cargo fmt --all -- --check` — passed.
- `cargo check --all-targets` — passed.
- Focused migration, app-switch, and retry tests — passed.
- `cargo test --lib --no-fail-fast -- --test-threads=1` — 427 passed, 0 failed.
- `cargo run --manifest-path xtask/Cargo.toml -- test-full-workflow` — 1 passed.
- `cargo run --manifest-path xtask/Cargo.toml -- test-multi-instance-sync` — 1 passed.
- Final `git status --short` — clean.

Goal tracker usage: 270,098 tokens over about 24 minutes 36 seconds.
rounds.prompt: Preserve and build on Round 4 candidate 19461ba; do not replace its verified shared-consistency work. Correct only the three governance findings and the adjacent authority edge. First, migrate actual pre-change workflow records whose legacy target_app_id or target_root canonical path proves ownership by the currently attached target root, rewriting that ownership to the stable app ID. If ownership cannot be proven and a legacy record contains any active Claimed or Running claim or unresolved pause decision, do not create empty competing authority or permit automation: preserve the record and return an actionable reconciliation conflict. Add regression evidence for both path-owned migration across runtime roots and ambiguous active legacy state refusing to fork. Second, make retry a launchable supervised workflow commitment. It must not publish Running, acquire lasting capacity, or return success unless a managed worker is actually launched; use the existing scheduler and process substrate, with deterministic recovery across crash or launch failure. Third, exercise cancellation versus late provider settlement and retry through the real daemon, workflow runner, and provider process rather than direct in-process WorkflowEngine or WorkflowContext calls. Prove the retry API causes provider work to resume under a managed process, reaches a terminal state, and releases capacity. Preserve all Round 4 tests and the full-workflow and two-daemon harnesses. Do not expand into Quality, Supervisor, UI, or unrelated cleanup.

failed_actions.message: Recovery runs only when a workflow runner first observes a target, and claim_has_live_owner treats any workflow runner in the owning runtime as proof that a specific claim is live. If that claim's provider or settlement is lost after another daemon has attached, the surviving daemon never rechecks it; subsequent evaluation ignores Running claims, leaving the Goal, claim, and capacity evidence stranded.
failed_actions.rule: Keep the daemon and surfaces responsive by running agent, Git, quality, lifecycle, and other long-running work through the supervised process substrate with explicit ownership, status, logs, cancellation, failure feedback, and recovery.

failed_actions.message: After checking state conflicts, the non-fast-forward push retry fetches and rebases the prepared state commit without recomputing semantic conflicts or consulting conflict evidence. Simultaneous Nodes changing different lines of the same authoritative record can therefore cleanly Git-rebase and publish a combined result without explicit conflict review.
failed_actions.rule: Coordinate parallel or distributed work explicitly through Node ownership, workflow claims and capacity, dependency or Feature ordering, and isolated Git branches or worktrees so concurrent changes remain reviewable and recoverable.

failed_actions.message: The added multi-daemon test verifies startup observation while the provider remains live, then allows the owner to settle normally; it does not test owner/provider loss while the observing daemon stays attached. Git conflict tests also synchronize Nodes sequentially rather than racing their pushes, leaving both defects unverified.
failed_actions.rule: Require evidence proportional to actual architectural and operational risk. Verify the affected behavior with the smallest focused checks that provide confidence, including integration or failure-path evidence when a change alters concurrency, persistence, process, Git, release, recovery, or cross-surface semantics. Update only affected docs/intent pages when documented product intent or an architectural contract changes; do not require unrelated documentation or broad cleanup.

rounds.implementation_report:
Implemented and committed Round 6 as `bfdfc92` (`Complete cross-process workflow coordination`). The worktree is clean and reviewable.

Changes:

- Atomically revalidates Goal revision, status, eligibility, ownership, workflow state, and capacity at claim/start/retry boundaries.
- Makes retry rollback a durable, crash-repairable commitment.
- Records runtime ownership and preserves live providers observed by simultaneous daemons; losing schedulers no longer fail the winning claim.
- Releases and signals processes/capacity through the owning runtime, while legacy ambiguous ownership blocks unsafe takeover.
- Persists Git conflict observations so unequal revisions remain conflicted until an explicit post-conflict edit.
- Derives target identity from configured `git_remote`, preserving existing identity records.
- Expands the real multi-daemon test for simultaneous workflow runners, duplicate-launch prevention, capacity ownership, and unequal-revision conflict resolution.

Verification:

- `git diff --check` — passed.
- `cargo fmt --all -- --check` — passed.
- `cargo check --all-targets` — passed.
- Six focused coordination, recovery, conflict, and identity tests — all passed.
- `cargo test --lib --no-fail-fast -- --test-threads=1` — 431 passed, 0 failed.
- `cargo run --manifest-path xtask/Cargo.toml -- test-full-workflow` — 1 passed, 0 failed.
- `cargo run --manifest-path xtask/Cargo.toml -- test-multi-instance-sync` — 1 passed, 0 failed.
- Final `git status --short` — clean.
rounds.prompt: Preserve and build on Round 5 candidate 4da89bd; do not replace its verified migration, retry, settlement, app-switch, and daemon-provider work. Correct only the remaining shared-consistency violations. 1. At the actual claim, promote, retry, and start commitment boundary, revalidate the Goal workflow_revision, status, eligibility, ownership, and relevant workflow-state version under the shared coordination model. A Goal mutation that wins first must make the stale workflow decision fail without publishing Claimed or Running state, acquiring capacity, or rolling the Goal back. Make retry rollback plus its linked claim recoverable as one commitment, with deterministic repair of any partial evidence. 2. Recovery must never fail or interrupt a claim merely because a second daemon or runtime observes the same target. Before changing an active Goal or claim, prove the owning supervised provider process is absent across the target-app runtime/process evidence; a live provider under another runtime remains authoritative. Preserve explicit ownership and add deterministic takeover only after absence is proven. 3. Preserve true Git conflicts regardless of unequal workflow_revision values. Revision magnitude alone is not evidence that a user reviewed a conflict. Reuse durable sync baseline/conflict evidence so only a new authoritative edit made after observing that specific conflict resolves it; blind retries and pre-existing unequal offline edits must remain conflicted while unrelated records converge. 4. Derive target-app identity from the configured git_remote rather than hard-coded origin, while preserving compatible existing identity records. Prove independent clones using a renamed or non-origin configured remote converge on the same identity. 5. Extend the smallest production-shaped evidence to run two workflow-enabled daemons simultaneously against the same target and Node, keep a real managed provider alive in one while the other performs startup recovery, and prove no duplicate launch, failure, interruption, or capacity theft. Add a concurrent unequal-revision same-Goal conflict test, then an explicit post-conflict edit that resolves it. Preserve all prior focused, serial, full-workflow, and two-daemon gates. Do not expand into Quality, Supervisor, UI, or unrelated cleanup.

failed_actions.message: The scheduler selects every Claimed record for the target app without filtering by the active Node. A daemon on Node B can therefore select Node A's claim; start_claim rejects the ownership mismatch, after which the scheduler marks Node A's durable claim Failed. Recovery reconcilers likewise inspect and mutate claims without consistently restricting them to the active Node.
failed_actions.rule: Coordinate parallel or distributed work explicitly through Node ownership, workflow claims and capacity, dependency or Feature ordering, and isolated Git branches or worktrees so concurrent changes remain reviewable and recoverable.

failed_actions.message: The daemon-backed coordination test deliberately runs both automation daemons as the same default Node. It does not exercise distinct active Nodes sharing synchronized workflow state, so it misses the off-node scheduling and recovery mutation path.
failed_actions.rule: Require evidence proportional to actual architectural and operational risk. rounds.implementation_report:
Completed Goal `00000SZ2HZHQX2TXXQ7SXVA00D` and committed the reviewable candidate as `ec0d336` (`Repair continuous workflow coordination`).

Changes:

- Continuously reconciles Running claims using exact provider or settlement ownership.
- Makes recovery ordering crash-safe and repairs stranded terminal-Goal claims and capacity.
- Recomputes semantic conflicts before non-fast-forward rebase or publication.
- Adds real daemon owner-loss and concurrent Git-push race coverage.
- Preserves the replayed `bfdfc92` candidate history beneath the new commit.

Verification:

- Focused recovery/conflict tests: 4 passed.
- `cargo test --lib --no-fail-fast -- --test-threads=1`: 434 passed, 0 failed.
- Full-workflow harness: 1 passed.
- Multi-instance harness: 1 passed.
- `cargo check --all-targets`: passed.
- Formatting and diff checks: passed.
- Independent governance re-review: passed.
- Final worktree: clean.

rounds.prompt: Preserve candidate bfdfc92f996d8a0f197cb740969eb42960ecf7f3 and fix the three Round 6 governance violations without introducing another recovery engine. (1) Make recovery continuously reconcile each Running claim against its exact supervised provider or settlement ownership, including owner/provider loss after another daemon is already attached; do not treat any workflow runner in the runtime as proof that a specific claim is live, and ensure stranded Goal, claim, and capacity state is recoverable. (2) On non-fast-forward durable-state push retry, re-read remote authoritative state and recompute semantic conflicts and conflict evidence before any rebase/publish; a clean Git rebase must not silently combine concurrent decisions on the same authoritative record. (3) Add proportional production-path tests for owner/provider loss while a second daemon remains attached and genuinely concurrent Node pushes that would otherwise cleanly rebase, proving explicit conflict review and no stranded capacity. Preserve the shared transaction, stable app identity, isolated worktrees, supervised processes, existing passing behavior, and the current candidate history. Run focused recovery/conflict tests, the serial library suite, full workflow, multi-instance sync, fmt, all-target check, diff checks, and independent governance.
